title: identity — model relationships
description: Identity is a GLOBAL root aggregate — it has NO outbound FKs. Application, Tenant, and User all reference Identity, never the reverse. Inbound FKs from `user::User`, `user::PlatformUser`, `mfa::MfaChallenge`, `auth::PasswordResetToken`, and `auth::EmailVerificationToken`. This module ships the Identity model only; the inbound-FK models are owned by the referencing modules and shown here for context.

internal.description: Password history is stored inline as a JSONB column (`password_history_hashed`) on `identities`, not as a separate table. This entry is retained in the blueprint for documentation clarity — in practice consumers read the JSONB blob via the `password_history` accessor on the Identity model.

outbound.description: Identity has zero outbound FKs. It NEVER references Application, Tenant, or User. Downstream aggregation lives on the referencing tables (`users.identity_id`, `platform_users.identity_id`) so the Identity row stays cross-Application by construction. This is the load-bearing invariant of design.md D1 — breaking it collapses the identity/user split.

inbound.description: Per-Application projection of an Identity. UNIQUE(identity_id, application_id) enforces one User per Identity per Application. Restrict on delete so a soft-deleted Identity keeps its User rows for GDPR hold-period audit.
Cross-tenant, cross-application platform-admin projection. Same Identity substrate as tenant Users; the platform_admin guard boundary lives on the PlatformUser row.
Short-lived (3-attempt / 5-minute TTL) MFA verification challenge. Cascade on hard delete once the identity row is genuinely purged past the GDPR hold-period.
Short-lived (15-minute TTL) reset token. One-time-use, cascade-purged on identity hard delete.
24-hour TTL verification token issued at registration + on email change.

accessor_chain.description: Identity exposes computed accessors that downstream code reads instead of touching JSONB columns directly.

Identity.is_locked: locked_until IS NOT NULL AND locked_until > now()
Identity.recovery_codes_remaining: count of non-consumed entries in mfa_recovery_codes_hashed JSONB
Identity.password_history: decoded array of prior bcrypt/argon2id hashes for reuse-prevention checks
